perturbation.original_text: Sponsor shall make a non-refundable deposit upon execution of this Agreement of five-hundred-thousand U.S. dollars (USD $500,000.00) (the "Deposit"), which shall be applied against the Sponsorship Fee for the first Contract Year.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor shall make a deposit upon execution of this Agreement of five-hundred-thousand U.S. dollars (USD $500,000.00) (the "Deposit"), which may or may not be refundable at the discretion of Forty Niners SC, and shall be applied against the Sponsorship Fee for the first Contract Year.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement specifies that the deposit is non-refundable. By modifying the clause to state that the deposit "may or may not be refundable at the discretion of Forty Niners SC", it introduces uncertainty and contradicts the previous definite statement. This creates an in-text contradiction within Section 5(b), which could lead to disputes over the deposit's refundability. The section originally stating the Deposit is non-refundable, now becomes ambiguous due to the insertion of 'may or may not be refundable at the discretion of Forty Niners SC'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor shall make a deposit upon execution of this Agreement of five-hundred-thousand U.S. dollars (USD $500,000.00) (the "Deposit"), which may or may not be refundable at the discretion of Forty Niners SC, and shall be applied against the Sponsorship Fee for the first Contract Year.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the deposit was "non-refundable", which is a definitive statement. The changed text alters this to "may or may not be refundable at the discretion of Forty Niners SC", introducing ambiguity and directly contradicting the initial certainty. This ambiguity concerning refundability, now residing within the same clause (Contradicted Text being a copy of the Changed Text in this instance), creates a clear in-text contradiction under the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition. The initial obligation is undermined, leading to uncertainty in the Sponsor's rights regarding the deposit. This corresponds to a Structural Flaw as well, because the original text was unambiguous in it's placement, and the change in placement introduces conflicting implications regarding refundability.

perturbation.original_text: Forty Niners SC acknowledges and agrees that, except as otherwise provided herein, the rights granted to Sponsor herein are exclusive to Sponsor within the Product and Services Category with respect to Forty Niners SC at the Stadium.
perturbation.changed_text: Forty Niners SC acknowledges and agrees that the rights granted to Sponsor herein may or may not be exclusive to Sponsor within the Product and Services Category with respect to Forty Niners SC at the Stadium, as determined solely by Forty Niners SC.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the phrase to "may or may not be exclusive to Sponsor within the Product and Services Category with respect to Forty Niners SC at the Stadium, as determined solely by Forty Niners SC," this modification introduces ambiguity. The original clause intended to give the Sponsor exclusive rights now includes an element of doubt and allows Forty Niners SC to potentially undermine that exclusivity. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty over Sponsor's exclusivity rights within the defined category. The section originally stating the sponsorship rights were exlusive, but the insertion of 'may or may not be exclusive' causes the contradiction within this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Forty Niners SC acknowledges and agrees that the rights granted to Sponsor herein may or may not be exclusive to Sponsor within the Product and Services Category with respect to Forty Niners SC at the Stadium, as determined solely by Forty Niners SC. Forty Niners SC shall not enter into a sponsorship agreement with a party with respect to the Product and Services Category, provided however, that Forty Niners SC shall be permitted to enter into a sponsorship agreement with any party that enters into a naming rights agreement with SCSA for the Stadium, provided that if SCSA enters into a naming rights agreement for the Stadium with a party that is in the Products and Services Category, Sponsor may immediately terminate this Agreement and receive a pro rated refund of any amounts paid by Sponsor for the unexpired Contract Year in which the termination occurs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating the rights granted to the sponsor 'may or may not be exclusive', while the contradicted text uses this same ambiguous language, potentially leading to disputes over the sponsor's exclusivity. The original text intended for the sponsorship to be exclusive, but now, after the change and contradicted text, it causes a contradiction in whether or not that exclusivity is guaranteed. This contradicts the original intention and creates an inconsistency.
